pdf_candidates.text_excerpt: Chemosphere 77 (2009) 863–869 Contents lists available at ScienceDirect Chemosphere journal homepage: www.elsevier.com/locate/chemosphere Speciﬁc proﬁles of perﬂuorinated compounds in surface and drinking waters and accumulation in mussels, ﬁsh, and dolphins from southeastern Brazil Natalia Quinete a,b, Qian Wu b, Tao Zhang b,c, Se Hun Yun b, Isabel Moreira a, Kurunthachalam Kannan b,* a Department of Chemistry, Pontifícia Universidade Católica do Rio de Janeiro (PUC-Rio), Rua Marquês de São Vicente, 225, Gávea-Rio de Janeiro, RJ-22453-900, Brazil b Wadsworth Center, New York State Department of Health and Department of Environmental Health Sciences, School of Public Health, State University of New York at Albany, Empire State Plaza, PO Box 509, Albany, NY 12201-0509, USA c Key Laboratory of Pollution Processes and Environmental Criteria, College of Environmental Science and Engineering, Nankai University, Tianjin 300071, China a r t i c l e i n f o a b s t r a c t Article history: Despite the concern over widespread distribution of perﬂuorinated compounds (PFCs) even in sparsely Received 26 May 2009 populated regions of the world, few studies have reported their occurrence in South America. In this Received in revised form 20 July 2009 study, PFCs were measured in Rio de Janeiro State in southeast Brazil: in drinking water from various dis- Accepted 28 July 2009 tricts in the State, in river water and tucuxi dolphins from the Paraiba do Sul River, several species of ﬁsh Available online 9 September 2009 from the State, and mussels from Guanabara Bay. Liver, kidney, and muscle from ﬁshes were analyzed to enable an understanding of the tissue distribution of PFCs. PFOS, PFOA, and PFHxS were detected in all Keywords: drinking water samples in concentration ranges of 0.58–6.70, 0.35–2.82, and 0.15–1.00 ng L 1, respec- PFOS PFOA tively. The proﬁles of PFCs in drinking water from Brazil (with PFOS concentrations comparable to or Mussel higher than those of PFOA) were different from the proﬁles that have been reported for other countries. Accumulation In ﬁsh, concentrations of PFOS were, in general, higher in liver than in muscle. Concentrations of PFOA in Dolphin livers of ﬁsh were similar to or lower than ﬁsh muscle tissue concentrations. PFOS and PFOA were found Fish muscle in brown mussels from Guanabara Bay. Bioconcentration factors (BCFs) of PFOA calculated for mussels were higher than the BCFs calculated for ﬁshes. Elevated concentrations of PFUnDA (mean: 109 ± 17.4 ng g 1 wet weight) were found in mussels from certain locations within Guanabara Bay. Although PFCs were detected in all types of samples analyzed, the concentrations were generally lower than the concentrations reported for Japan and the USA. Ó 2009 Elsevier Ltd. All rights reserved. 1. Introduction 2008).
